skill.name: ml-antipattern-validator
skill.description: Reviews machine learning projects for data leakage, evaluation mistakes, training pitfalls, and deployment readiness issues.
skill.summary: Validate ML workflows for leakage, flawed evaluation, training mistakes, and production readiness gaps.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are mostly false positives from markdown examples, inline code formatting, regex strings, and legitimate model.eval() usage. The bundled scripts perform user-directed local file analysis and can print dataset-derived values, so publication is acceptable with low-risk disclosure.

low_findings.title: User-Directed Local File Parsing
low_findings.description: The included scripts read Python and CSV files from user-provided paths for local ML validation. This is expected behavior, but users should avoid sharing outputs from sensitive datasets because summaries can include dataset-derived values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scripts clearly load user-specified local files and print validation summaries. No network transmission or hidden file collection was found, so the risk is limited and user-directed.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby or shell backtick findings are caused by markdown code fences, inline command examples, and ML pseudocode in references. I found no executable shell invocation such as subprocess, os.system, or backtick execution in the scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation or fenced examples, not runnable marketplace code. Script review did not show command execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Static eval Findings Are Benign ML and Regex References
low_findings.description: The eval findings refer to PyTorch model.eval() guidance and regex text checking model.predict patterns. They do not execute dynamic code or evaluate attacker-controlled strings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: model.eval() is an inference-mode call, and the regex strings are static analysis patterns. There is no Python eval() or exec() call in the inspected code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Documentation Text False Positives
low_findings.description: Weak cryptography hits map to phrases such as leakage being the number one cause of invalid ML results or numbered checklist headings. I found no hashing or cryptographic implementation using weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are prose, checklist numbers, or argument parser setup. No MD5, SHA-1, encryption, signing, or password hashing code was found in reviewed files.

content.value_statement: Machine learning teams often miss leakage, flawed splits, and misleading metrics before release. This skill guides review and local checks for safer model evaluation and deployment.
actual_capabilities: Reviews ML workflows for target, temporal, preprocessing, group, and augmentation leakage.
Provides evaluation guidance for train, validation, and test separation.
Flags metric mismatches for classification, regression, ranking, and imbalanced data tasks.
Includes scripts that inspect Python ML code and validate CSV train-test splits.
Provides deployment checklists for drift monitoring, rollback planning, and retraining gates.
Supplies reference examples for common statistical, training, and architecture mistakes.
limitations: The scripts use heuristic checks and can produce false positives or miss project-specific leakage.
CSV validation requires users to provide correct date, group, and target column names.
The skill does not train models, fix pipelines automatically, or verify production systems directly.
It does not replace a domain expert review for regulated or high-stakes ML deployments.

use_cases.title: Audit a Training Pipeline
use_cases.description: Review feature engineering, preprocessing, splitting, and evaluation code before trusting model metrics.

use_cases.title: Check Dataset Splits
use_cases.description: Validate train and test CSV files for sample overlap, temporal leakage, group leakage, and distribution shift.

use_cases.title: Prepare Model Release Reviews
use_cases.description: Use deployment checklists to confirm monitoring, drift detection, rollback, and retraining plans.

prompt_templates.title: Review Basic ML Split
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a new dataset split.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my train and test split plan for data leakage. Focus on target, temporal, preprocessing, and group leakage risks.

prompt_templates.title: Validate Evaluation Metrics
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before reporting model results.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether my evaluation metrics match my ML task and business goal. Identify misleading metrics and better alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Training Code
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of model training and preprocessing code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this ML training workflow for leakage, test-set reuse, bad cross-validation, imbalance handling, and inference-mode mistakes.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Deployment Readiness
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert release gate for production ML systems.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this model deployment plan for drift monitoring, feedback loops, rollback readiness, retraining gates, and production validation gaps.

output_examples.input: A fraud model uses refund status as a feature and reports very high test accuracy.
output_examples.output: The review flags target leakage because refund status is only known after the fraud decision. It recommends removing post-event features and rebuilding the split.

output_examples.input: A time-series model uses a random train-test split and standard scaling before splitting.
output_examples.output: The review identifies temporal leakage and preprocessing leakage. It recommends time-ordered splits and fitting all preprocessing only on training data.

output_examples.input: A model release plan has no drift monitoring or rollback procedure.
output_examples.output: The review marks deployment readiness as incomplete. It recommends input drift checks, performance monitoring, alerting, and a tested rollback process.

best_practices: Split data before any preprocessing, feature selection, augmentation, or exploratory model tuning.
Use validation data for development decisions and reserve the test set for final evaluation only.
Document metrics, baselines, confidence intervals, drift monitors, and rollback steps before deployment.
anti_patterns: Reporting high accuracy on imbalanced data without per-class metrics or a baseline comparison.
Using random splits for time-series or grouped data where related records cross split boundaries.
Retuning models after seeing test results or production performance without a new validation protocol.

faq.question: Can this skill find every data leakage issue?
faq.answer: No. It combines checklists, examples, and heuristic scripts. Human review is still needed for domain-specific leakage.

faq.question: Does it execute external commands?
faq.answer: No executable shell command use was found in the reviewed scripts. Static command findings were markdown and example text false positives.

faq.question: What files can the included scripts read?
faq.answer: They read user-provided Python files or CSV train-test datasets from local paths. Users control which files are analyzed.

faq.question: Is it useful for deep learning projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers inference mode, overfitting, class imbalance, augmentation leakage, and deployment concerns relevant to deep learning.

faq.question: Can it replace MLOps monitoring tools?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan monitoring and review readiness, but it does not operate production monitoring systems.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, with prompts and scripts that support ML review workflows.
